description: Create a hierarchical 'Digital Humanities Research Guide' in WordPress by publishing a parent page and four child pages. The parent page 'Digital Humanities Research Guide' must contain 'digital humanities resources available to researchers'. The four child pages must be assigned to this parent with the following titles, menu orders, and content: 'Getting Started with Digital Humanities' (Order 1, 'introduction to the field of digital humanities'), 'Primary Source Databases' (Order 2, 'primary source collections and archival databases'), 'Digital Tools and Software' (Order 3, 'computational tools and software platforms'), and 'Citation and Attribution Guide' (Order 4, 'proper citation practices and attribution standards'). Ensure all pages are published.

metadata.parent_phrase: digital humanities resources available to researchers

children.title: Getting Started with Digital Humanities
children.phrase: introduction to the field of digital humanities

children.title: Primary Source Databases
children.phrase: primary source collections and archival databases

children.title: Citation and Attribution Guide
children.phrase: proper citation practices and attribution standards
